patient_information.patient_address: 18 N Ontario St, New York, NY 21022

clinical_information.diagnosis: High-grade osteosarcoma of the distal femur, status post surgical resection and adjuvant chemotherapy
clinical_information.prior_treatments_and_results: Neoadjuvant chemotherapy (March–April 2024): doxorubicin 75 mg/m2 IV q21 days, cisplatin 120 mg/m2 IV q21 days, high-dose methotrexate 12 g/m2 IV q14 days (3 cycles). Surgical resection (limb-sparing, distal femur, prosthetic implant) on May 1, 2024. Adjuvant chemotherapy (May–Sept 2024): doxorubicin 37.5 mg/m2 IV q21 days, cisplatin 90 mg/m2 IV q21 days, high-dose methotrexate 12 g/m2 IV q14 days, ifosfamide 9 g/m2 IV q21 days with Mesna (6 cycles). No imaging response results documented in the note; patient reports postoperative left thigh pain.
clinical_information.lab_summary: Key abnormalities: pancytopenia with leukopenia (WBC 2.5 x10^9/L), anemia (Hgb 9.5 g/dL, Hct 28%), and thrombocytopenia (platelets 75 x10^9/L) — increased infection and bleeding risk. Hepatic abnormalities with mild-to-moderate transaminitis and cholestatic pattern (AST 55 U/L, ALT 60 U/L, ALP 150 U/L) plus markedly elevated LDH (450 U/L). Low albumin (3.0 g/dL) and markedly elevated ferritin (800 ng/mL) suggest significant inflammation, iron overload, or macrophage activation/hemophagocytic process. Renal function, electrolytes, glucose and coagulation (PT/aPTT/INR) are within reference ranges; beta-hCG negative. Overall pattern is concerning for bone marrow suppression/infiltration, severe systemic inflammatory state or liver disease; further evaluation with peripheral smear, reticulocyte count, iron studies, inflammatory markers (CRP/ESR), viral serologies and hematology/liver workup (consider bone marrow biopsy) is recommended.
clinical_information.imaging_results:
CT chest (09/04/24, non-contrast): 1.5 cm rounded, spiculated lesion with irregular margins in the left lower lobe suspicious for metastasis; multiple small pulmonary nodules up to 5 mm in both lungs; no mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy; no pleural effusion or pleural thickening.

MRI left femur (09/04/24, T1/T2/STIR): New irregular mass at the prior osteosarcoma resection site measuring approximately 4.0 cm with heterogeneous signal and T2 hyperintense areas consistent with edema and possible necrosis; tumor extends into surrounding soft tissues with irregular margins and adjacent muscle edema; extensive surrounding bone marrow edema; thickening of surrounding soft tissues consistent with a desmoplastic reaction; no new suspicious lesions identified in the proximal femur or surrounding structures.

X-ray left knee (03/01/24, AP and lateral): Aggressive-appearing lytic bone lesion in the distal femur approximately 5 cm in greatest dimension with cortical destruction and a soft tissue mass extending into the adjacent knee joint; periosteal reaction with a "sunburst" pattern; no significant joint effusion; surrounding soft tissues thickened; no evidence of metastatic disease in surrounding bones or soft tissues.
clinical_information.symptom_severity_and_impact: Patient reports left thigh/knee pain at prior resection site and non-productive cough and fatigue for the last 2 weeks. Severity and functional impact are not quantified in the note.

treatment_request.dosage: 5mg/m2 once daily (oral tablet); Take 5mg/m2 once daily

decision.reason: The submitted request for everolimus does not meet the UnitedHealthcare Afinitor (everolimus) osteosarcoma initial authorization criteria. Policy requires all of the following for approval: (1) diagnosis of osteosarcoma, (2) relapsed/refractory or metastatic disease, (3) used as second-line therapy, AND (4) used in combination with Nexavar (sorafenib). Although the patient has relapsed/metastatic osteosarcoma, the request documents everolimus as monotherapy and does not document use in combination with sorafenib. Additionally, the request does not explicitly document that everolimus is being used as second-line therapy per the policy language. Therefore the request fails to meet required criterion (4) (and lacks explicit documentation for criterion (3)).
